title: helichrysum panduratum var. transvaalense
intro: Helichrysum panduratum var. transvaalense, also known as the Transvaal Everlasting, is a perennial shrub native to South Africa, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Helichrysum panduratum var. transvaalense are typically used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally.
